source.attribution: Official source pages and configured public event feeds

events.title: Desert Oasis Summer at the Garden Kickoff Weekend
events.description: Three nights. Music. Lights. Desert vibes done right. At the Garden we don’t run from summer. We celebrate it. After sunset, enjoy a made-for-summer Desert Oasis Garden After Dark experience as more than 12 light installations take over the trails. This shimmering display creates a playful break from the summer heat with cooling colors and designs inspired b

events.title: Mark Klett: Desert Citizens
events.description: Experience the Sonoran Desert through the lens of award-winning Arizona landscape photographer Mark Klett in this photography exhibit. With a career spanning more than four decades, Klett reframes our notions of time, cultural memory and the land around us. “The saguaro only grows in the Sonoran Desert in Arizona and Sonora, Mexico. They are magnificent plan

events.title: It’s Superhero Month!
events.description: Heroes come in many forms, and this month, we’re celebrating every kind of hero through fun, interactive programs! June’s engaging and educational programs are generously supported by First Things First .

events.title: Keith Haring Heart Collages
events.description: Be inspired by artist Keith Haring as you assemble your own “You Gotta Have Heart” collage!

events.title: Postcards for The Great Indoors
events.description: Stop by Star Bar on the 2nd floor and design a one-of-a-kind postcard to remember your Museum experience! Using the supplies provided, draw a picture or decorate the front with your favorite moment, then flip it over to write or draw a message to send to a friend or loved one, or keep it as a personal keepsake.

events.title: Desert Oasis Garden After Dark
events.description: Enjoy this made-for-summer Garden After Dark experience as more than 12 light installations take over the trails. This shimmering display creates a playful break from the summer heat with cooling colors and designs inspired by the monsoon season.

events.title: Soundless Socials: Desert Oasis Garden After Dark
events.description: Each Sunday evening, enjoy Soundless Socials (Quiet Night) as you stroll along the Garden trails all while you enjoy 12 immersive light experiences at Desert Oasis Garden After Dark

events.title: Shadows of Strength
events.description: Not all heroes shine in the spotlight; some move in quieter ways. Kids will bring heroes to life by creating shadow puppets and exploring unseen acts of bravery. Discover how strength can exist both in the light and in the shadows.

events.title: 80s Music Night: Desert Oasis Garden After Dark
events.description: Let the music set the mood for your Desert Oasis Garden After Dark experience. Each Monday evening, enjoy 80s music along the Garden trails all while you enjoy 12 immersive light experiences Gardenwide.

events.title: Desert Oasis Garden After Dark
events.description: Enjoy this made-for-summer Garden After Dark experience as more than 12 light installations take over the trails. This shimmering display creates a playful break from the summer heat with cooling colors and designs inspired by the monsoon season.

events.title: Community Day: Pay What you Can Day
events.description: Your Garden, Your Way! We believe nature should be accessible to everyone. On Community Day pay what you can. Pay $5, $10, $15 or any amount that works for you. In return, your contribution nurtures the Garden’s research, conservation and protection of desert plants for generations to come. events.title: Cooking with Krystal

events.title: Try it-Tuesday: The Ripple Effect
events.description: What happens when a heroic act sends waves of change through a community? In this hands-on exploration, kids will experiment with water and movement to see how actions create ripples of impact. Curious minds will discover that even the smallest heroic act can make a huge difference.

events.title: Juneteenth
events.description: The Garden is celebrating Juneteenth (June 19) on Community Day with a vibrant celebration. A day of reflection, fun and connection, where we honor the enduring legacy of freedom, culture and community. Celebrate Juneteenth at the Garden with an evening filled with a variety of family-friendly activities, food trucks, community resources, cooking demos, stor

events.title: Wellness Wednesdays: Sunrise Yoga
events.description: Begin your morning connecting with nature during this memorable Sunrise Yoga session, led by Metta Yoga. Together, in a Sonoran Desert oasis, breathe, stretch and embrace the cooler morning air whilst enjoying Garden views. This is an all-levels class. events.title: Little Rhythms- Heroic Hearts
events.description: Little Rhythms is a month-long, cohort-style music workshop for toddlers (ages 2–3) and their caregivers. Families explore musical styles connected to the museum’s monthly themes and character skills while building connections and developing early literacy and STEAM skills through play. events.title: Early Connections
events.description: Held in our cozy Room for Threes and Younger exhibit, a program educator will be present to offer age‑appropriate provocations designed to spark curiosity and support early development. events.title: Eye on the Wild
events.description: Step into The Great Indoors for a camping-inspired adventure! Young explorers will craft their own binoculars, then set off on a room-wide scavenger hunt to spot hidden “wildlife” and nature-inspired surprises. No bugs or mud, just a pure indoor adventure!

events.title: International Day of Play
events.description: It’s a day all about PLAY! Play helps us get curious and creative, helps us learn how to be kind and respectful, and even teaches us how to be brave. Join us in the Atrium as we celebrate International Day of Play. Help build obstacle courses, jump, hop, twirl, create, collaborate, and, most importantly…PLAY!

events.title: Bridges of Impact
events.description: Our city heroes have an urgent mission! Nurse Jerry needs to get from his house to the hospital to help patients in need, but the bridge is broken! Be a city hero and help build a bridge over the river so our heroes can continue caring for the community. It takes bravery to step up and help solve problems

events.title: Hero Care Kit
events.description: We are our own heroes, and every hero needs to take care of themselves. In this creative session, kids will explore what fills their hearts and calms their minds as they build their own “hero care kit.” They’ll explore a variety of items and create a kit that helps them feel cared for. The first act of heroism is caring for yourself.

events.title: Dog Days of Summer
events.description: Beat the heat with your four-legged best friend. Early mornings are made for wagging tails and cool trails, and the Garden opens its paths for pups and their people to explore the desert together. events.title: Flashlight Nights: Summer Edition
events.description: Whether you’re planning a date night or a family adventure, discover the magic of Flashlight Nights. events.description: Join local origami artist, Charlie Humme, as he teaches us how to make our very own origami collared shirts to celebrate Father’s Day! Make and share with the loved ones who care for you.

events.title: Banana Pudding with Edible Ed!
events.description: Sessions at 10:30 & 12:00 Join Edible Ed in the KIDchen, as we celebrate Juneteenth with some delicious Banana Pudding! Banana pudding is a beloved and cherished dessert in many households, and is often associated with family gatherings and celebrations like Juneteenth. events.description: Heroes come in many forms, and this month, we’re celebrating every kind of hero through fun, interactive programs! June’s engaging and educational programs are generously supported by First Things First .

events.title: Juneteenth Mosaic Flags
events.description: Learn about Juneteenth and the two different flags used to celebrate this important holiday! Stop by the Art Studio this week to make your own mosaic Juneteenth flag!

events.title: Postcards for The Great Indoors
events.description: Stop by Star Bar on the 2nd floor and design a one-of-a-kind postcard to remember your Museum experience! Using the supplies provided, draw a picture or decorate the front with your favorite moment, then flip it over to write or draw a message to send to a friend or loved one, or keep it as a personal keepsake.

events.title: Shields of Freedom with Diamin Robinson
events.description: Designed and facilitated by Diamin Robinson, this Juneteenth-inspired hands-on activity invites kids to connect a favorite superhero theme with history by exploring Freedom, Joy, and Unity as real-life superpowers. Children create protective shields that symbolize caring for others and building strong, resilient communities.

events.title: Soul Plates with Chef Krystal
events.description: Come cook with Chef Krystal this Friday for a special Juneteenth-inspired culinary experience that blends historical and cultural learning with hands-on STEAM cooking. Students will prepare mini “soul plates” featuring garden collard greens, cornbread muffins, and smoky black-eyed peas.

events.title: “Sonic Boom” Freedom Shakers with Diamin Robinson
events.description: Designed and facilitated by Diamin Robinson, this Juneteenth program brings kids and families together to explore how sound can spark joy and connection. Children create their own “freedom shakers” and join in fun, high-energy music-making. Take inspiration from the drums, rhythms, and celebrations that welcomed emancipation. Power up your sound and fill the

events.title: Origami with the Japanese Friendship Garden
events.description: Join our friends from the Japanese Friendship Garden to turn simple sheets into beautiful creations! They will guide families in crafting origami pieces that celebrate both creativity and Japanese craftsmanship. Explore shape, design, and cultural appreciation in this hands‑on workshop on the 2nd floor.

events.title: Members-Only Extended Play Hours
events.description: Members-Only Extended Play Hours take place on the third Friday of each month, from 4pm-7pm. Join today and begin receiving benefits like this, and so much more right away!

events.title: Desert Oasis Garden After Dark
events.description: Enjoy this made-for-summer Garden After Dark experience as more than 12 light installations take over the trails. This shimmering display creates a playful break from the summer heat with cooling colors and designs inspired by the monsoon season.

events.title: Latin Music Nights: Desert Oasis Garden After Dark

events.title: Dog Days of Summer
events.description: Beat the heat with your four-legged best friend. Early mornings are made for wagging tails and cool trails, and the Garden opens its paths for pups and their people to explore the desert together. Curious minds will discover that even the smallest heroic act can make a huge difference.

events.title: Hero Care Kit
events.description: We are our own heroes, and every hero needs to take care of themselves. In this creative session, kids will explore what fills their hearts and calms their minds as they build their own “hero care kit.” They’ll explore a variety of items and create a kit that helps them feel cared for. The first act of heroism is caring for yourself.

events.title: Dog Days of Summer
events.description: Beat the heat with your four-legged best friend. Early mornings are made for wagging tails and cool trails, and the Garden opens its paths for pups and their people to explore the desert together. events.title: Community Day: Pay What you Can Day
events.description: Your Garden, Your Way! We believe nature should be accessible to everyone. On Community Day pay what you can. Pay $5, $10, $15 or any amount that works for you.
